question.text: Lakshmi, a 62-year-old retiree, has Rs.1,20,00,000 in corpus. She needs Rs.60,000 per month. Which of the following is the most prudent allocation strategy?

answer.text: 100% in equity mutual funds for highest returns
70% in safe instruments (FDs, SCSS, MIS) and 30% in balanced/equity funds
100% in gold bars stored at home
50% in cryptocurrency and 50% in real estate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For a 62-year-old retiree, 70% in safe instruments ensures predictable income for essential needs, while 30% in balanced or equity funds provides inflation protection. This aligns with the retirement stage guideline of maximum 25-30% in growth assets. **The Trap:** 100% equity is too risky for a retiree. 100% in any single asset class lacks diversification. Crypto and gold-at-home carry additional risks of volatility and security. **Cross-Exam Context:** The 70-30 debt-equity split for retirees is consistent with the lifecycle allocation framework (25-30% max in growth assets during retirement). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Implement the 70-30 split using specific products: SCSS (up to Rs.30L), Post Office MIS, bank FDs for the safe portion; balanced advantage funds or equity hybrid funds for the growth portion.
